title: Multiply and divide whole numbers by 10, 100, and 1000
description: Practice multiplying and dividing whole numbers by 10, 100, and 1000.
topic_path: Multiplying and dividing whole numbers by 10, 100, and 1000
Multiply and divide whole numbers by 10, 100, and 1000

hints: Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$259$ |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | | $2$ | $5$ | $9$
$25{,}900$ | $2$ | $5$ | $,$ | $9$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $259$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $259$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
259 \blueD{\times 10 \times 10} &=25{,}900 \\\\
259 \blueD{\times100} &= 25{,}900
\end{align}$

Number | Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$259$ | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$| $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | | $2$ | $5$ | $9$
$259{,}000$ | $2$ | $5$ |$9$ |  $,$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $259$ moved $3$ places to the left. So we multiplied $259$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
259 \maroonD{\times 10 \times 10 \times 10} &=259{,}000 \\\\
259 \maroonD{\times1{,}000} &= 259{,}000
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
259 \blueD{\times 100} &= 25{,}900\\\\
259 \maroonD{\times 1{,}000} &= 259{,}000
\end{align}$
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$19$ |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ ||$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $1$ | $9$
$19{,}000$ | $1$ | $9$ | $,$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $19$ moved $3$ places to the left. So we multiplied $19$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
19 \blueD{\times 10 \times 10\times 10} &=19{,}000 \\\\
19 \blueD{\times1{,}000} &= 19{,}000
\end{align}$

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$19$ | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | $1$ | $9$ 
$190$ | $1$ | $9$ |$0$ 

The digits in $19$ moved $1$ place to the left. So we multiplied $19$ by $1$ factor of $10$.

$19 \maroonD{\times 10 } =190$

$\begin{align}
19 \blueD{\times 1{,}000} &= 19{,}000\\\\
19 \maroonD{\times 10} &= 190
\end{align}$
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$6$ |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $6$
$600$ | $6$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $6$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $6$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
6 \blueD{\times 10 \times 10} &=600 \\\\
6 \blueD{\times100} &= 600
\end{align}$

:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-:

$6{,}000$ | $6$ | $,$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $6$ moved $3$ places to the left. So we multiplied $6$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

6 \maroonD{\times 10 \times 10 \times 10} &=60{,}000 \\\\
6 \maroonD{\times1{,}000} &= 6{,}000

$\begin{align}

$\begin{align}
410 \blueD{\times 10 \times 10 \times10} &=410{,}000 \\\\

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$73$ |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $7$ | $3$
$730$ | $7$ | $3$ | $0$

The digits in $73$ moved $1$ place to the left. So we multiplied $73$ by $1$ factor of $10$.

$73 \blueD{\times 10 } =730$

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: 
$73$ | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | $7$ | $3$ 
$7{,}300$ | $7$ | $,$ | $3$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $73$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $73$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
73 \maroonD{\times 10  \times 10} &=73{,}000 \\\\
73 \maroonD{\times100} &= 7{,}300
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
73 \blueD{\times 10 }&=730\\\\
73 \maroonD{\times100} &= 7{,}300
\end{align}$
Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

Number | Thousands | | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$4{,}400$ |$4$| $,$| $4$ | $0$ | $0$
$440$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ |  | $4$ | $4$ | $0$

The digits in $4{,}400$ moved $1$ place value to the right. So we divided $4{,}400$ by $1$ divisor of $10$.

$\begin{align}
4{,}400 \maroonD{\div 10} &=440 
\end{align}$

Number | Thousands |  | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: | :-: 
$4{,}400$ |$4$| $,$| $4$ | $0$ | $0$ 
$44$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ |  | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ | $4$ |$4$

The digits in $4{,}400$ moved $2$ place values to the right. So we divided $4{,}400$ by $2$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
4{,}400 \blueD{\div 10}\blueD{\div 10} &=44 \\\\
4{,}400 \blueD{\div 100} &=44 
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
4{,}400 \maroonD{\div 10} &=440 \\\\
4{,}400 \blueD{\div 100} &=44 
\end{align}$
Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: | :-: 
$8{,}000$ |$8$| $,$| $0$ | $0$ | $0$ 
$8$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ |  | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$  |$\blueD{\rightarrow}$| $8$ 

The digits in $8{,}000$ moved $3$ places to the right. So we divided $8{,}000$ by $3$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
8{,}000 \blueD{\div 10}\blueD{\div 10}\blueD{\div 10} &=8\\\\
8{,}000 \blueD{\div 1{,}000} &=8
\end{align}$

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$8{,}000$ |$8$| $,$| $0$ | $0$ | $0$
$80$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ |  | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | $8$ | $0$

The digits in $8{,}000$ moved $2$ places to the right. So we divided $8{,}000$ by $2$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
8{,}000 \maroonD{\div 10} \maroonD{\div 10} &=80 \\\\
8{,}000 \maroonD{\div 100} &=80 
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
8{,}000 \blueD{\div 1{,}000} &=8 \\\\
8{,}000 \maroonD{\div 100} &=80 
\end{align}$
Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

$53{,}000$ |$5$| $3$ |$,$  | $0$| $0$| $0$
$53$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ |  $5$ | $,$ |$3$|  $0$| $0$

The digits in $53{,}000$ moved $1$ place value to the right. So we divided $53{,}000$ by $1$ divisor of $10$.

$53{,}000 \blueD{\div 10} =5{,}300$

Number | Ten Thousands| Thousands| |Hundreds| Tens | Ones 
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  | :-: | :-: 
$53{,}000$ |$5$| $3$ |$,$  | $0$| $0$| $0$
$53$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ |  $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | |$\maroonD{\rightarrow}$| $5$ | $3$ 

The digits in $53{,}000$ moved $3$ place values to the right. So we divided $53{,}000$ by $3$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
53{,}000 \maroonD{\div 10}  \maroonD{\div 10} \maroonD{\div 10} &=53 \\\\
53{,}000 \maroonD{\div 1{,}000} &=53 
\end{align}$
Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the right**.

Number | Ten Thousands| Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$77{,}000$ |$7$| $7$| $,$| $0$ | $0$ | $0$
$770$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | | $7$| $7$ | $0$

The digits in $77{,}000$ moved $2$ places to the right. So we divided $77{,}000$ by $2$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
77{,}000 \maroonD{\div 10} \maroonD{\div 10} &=770 \\\\
77{,}000 \maroonD{\div 100} &=770
\end{align}$

Number | Ten Thousands| Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$77{,}000$ |$7$| $7$| $,$| $0$ | $0$ | $0$
$7{,}700$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ | $7$ | $,$ |$7$|  $0$ |$0$

The digits in $77{,}000$ moved $1$ place to the right. So we divided $77{,}000$ by $1$ divisor of $10$.

$77,000 \blueD{\div 10} =7{,}700$

$\begin{align}
77{,}000 \maroonD{\div 100} &=770 \\\\
77,000 \blueD{\div 10} &=7{,}700 
\end{align}$
